Container.Name: media-curator
Container.Overview:
media-curator audits your Radarr/Sonarr library for files whose size contradicts the quality they claim to be, and reclaims disk space by demoting over-quality files (e.g. 4K remuxes to 4K WEB-DL) using Radarr's native download-verify-replace path — it NEVER re-encodes. Free space is read from the filesystem (statvfs), not Radarr's per-share numbers, so it works correctly on unRAID/ZFS.
Configure Radarr and Sonarr in the WebUI (Connections) — no environment variables or config files needed. Set a free-space target and the loop holds it. Rule-based profile assignment (by genre, collection, or tag) keeps groups like kids movies on the right archive profile automatically. Protect anything with a blocklist and a keep-tag. Starts in dry-run.
IMPORTANT: mount your media share READ-ONLY at /media, pointed at the real dataset/share that holds your movies, so free space and orphan detection are accurate. media-curator never writes to it — Radarr performs the file operations.
Container.Description: Reclaim disk space in your Radarr/Sonarr library by demoting over-quality files (4K remux -> 4K WEB-DL, etc.) without re-encoding. Configure everything in the WebUI. Starts in dry-run.

Config.Name: Appdata (config + database)
Config.Description: Where media-curator stores its SQLite database, settings, and action history.

Config.Name: Media (read-only)
Config.Description: Your media share, mounted READ-ONLY. media-curator reads true free space (statvfs) and detects orphan folders here; it never writes. Point this at the real dataset/share that holds your movies (e.g. /mnt/cluster/Media on a ZFS pool) for accurate free-space readings.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Container timezone (affects scheduled run times shown in the UI).
